description: Add tags, notes, and met_via metadata to contacts + DM access control via contacts

userStories.title: Contact metadata — tags, notes, met_via
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to store tags, notes, and met_via on contacts so that I can remember context about how I know each contact.
acceptanceCriteria: contacts table has new columns: tags TEXT DEFAULT '[]', notes TEXT DEFAULT '', met_via TEXT DEFAULT ''
ContactAddRequest model has optional fields: tags (list[str]), notes (str), met_via (str)
ContactResponse model includes tags, notes, met_via
POST /contacts/ accepts and stores tags, notes, met_via
GET /contacts/ returns tags, notes, met_via
PATCH /contacts/{peer_id} endpoint added for updating metadata without re-adding
Tags are stored as JSON array string in SQLite
All existing tests pass
Add test for contact metadata CRUD
userStories.notes: DB schema: ALTER TABLE contacts ADD COLUMN tags TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'; ALTER TABLE contacts ADD COLUMN notes TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''; ALTER TABLE contacts ADD COLUMN met_via TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''; Models in common/models.py, routes in server/routes_contacts.py

userStories.title: DM access control — contacts-only by default
userStories.description: As an agent, I want DMs to only be allowed from contacts so that random unknown agents can't message me.
acceptanceCriteria: agents table has new column: dm_policy TEXT DEFAULT 'contacts_only'
When dm_policy is 'contacts_only', server rejects DMs from non-contacts with 403
When dm_policy is 'open', any agent can DM (backward compatible)
AgentRegisterRequest can optionally set dm_policy
GET /agents/me returns dm_policy
PATCH /agents/me endpoint for updating dm_policy
DM check happens in both HTTP send_message and WebSocket message handler
Error message clearly says 'Not in contacts. Ask the recipient to add you first.'
All existing tests pass (update tests that assume open DM to set dm_policy='open')
Add tests for contacts-only DM rejection and open DM
userStories.notes: Check in routes_messages.py send_message and routes_ws.py. For existing tests, register agents with dm_policy='open' to not break them. New default is 'contacts_only'.

userStories.title: Mutual contacts add API
userStories.description: As a Space server, I want to add mutual contacts between two agents in one API call so that matched agents can DM each other after the Space ends.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /contacts/mutual endpoint that takes {agent_a, agent_b, alias_a, alias_b, tags, met_via}
Adds both directions: A→B and B→A with respective aliases
Requires application-level auth (is_application=true) OR either agent's auth
Returns both contact records
Idempotent — re-calling updates instead of errors
All existing tests pass
Add test for mutual contacts creation
userStories.notes: This is for Space Server to call after matching. Space registers as an application agent (is_application=true). routes_contacts.py
